# rstcheck-core


Library for checking syntax of reStructuredText and code blocks nested within it.

See the full documentation at [read-the-docs]

```{contents}
```

## Installation

From pip

```shell
$ pip install rstcheck_core
```

To use pyproject.toml for configuration:

```
$ pip install rstcheck_core[toml]
```

To add sphinx support:

```
$ pip install rstcheck_core[sphinx]
```

To add YAML checking support:

```
$ pip install rstcheck_core[yaml]
```

## Supported languages in code blocks

- Bash
- Doctest
- C (C99)
- C++ (C++11)
- JSON
- XML
- Python
- reStructuredText
- YAML

modules.name: rstcheck_core._extras
documentation:
Central place for install-checker and guards for 'extras' dependencies.

The `*_INSTALLED` constansts reveal wether the dependency is installed with a supported version.
The {py:func}`install_guard` guard function is intended for use inside functions which need specific
extra packages installed.

Example usage:

```python
from rstcheck_core import _extras

if _extras.SPHINX_INSTALLED:
    import sphinx


def print_sphinx_version():
    _extras.install_guard("sphinx")
    print(sphinx.version_info)
```

functions.name: rstcheck_core._extras.is_installed_with_supported_version
documentation:
Check if the package is installed and has the minimum required version.

```
param package

Name of packge to check

return

Bool if package is installed with supported version
```

documentation:
Guard code that needs the `package` installed and throw {py:exc}`ModuleNotFoundError`.

See example in module docstring.

```

Name of packge to check

raises ModuleNotFoundError

When the package is not installed.
```

```

raises ModuleNotFoundError

When tomli is not installed.
```

functions.name: rstcheck_core.inline_config.find_ignored_directives
documentation:
Search the rst source for rstcheck inline ignore-directives comments.

Directives are ignored via comment.

For example, to ignore directive1, directive2, and directive3:

```
.. testsetup::

    from rstcheck_core.inline_config import find_ignored_directives
```

```pycon
>>> list(find_ignored_directives('''
... Example
... =======
...
... .. rstcheck: ignore-directives=directive1,directive3
...
... .. rstcheck: ignore-directives=directive2
... ''', "<string>"))
['directive1', 'directive3', 'directive2']
```

```
param source

Rst source code

param source_origin

Origin of the source with the inline ignore comments

return

None

yield

Found directives to ignore
```

functions.name: rstcheck_core.inline_config.find_ignored_roles
documentation:
Search the rst source for rstcheck inline ignore-roles comments.

Roles are ignored via comment.

For example, to ignore role1, role2, and role3:

```
.. testsetup::

    from rstcheck_core.inline_config import find_ignored_roles
```

```pycon
>>> list(find_ignored_roles('''
... Example
... =======
...
... .. rstcheck: ignore-roles=role1,role3
...
... .. rstcheck: ignore-roles=role2
... ''', "<string>"))
['role1', 'role3', 'role2']
```

```
param source

Rst source code

param source_origin

Origin of the source with the inline ignore comments

return

None

yield

Found roles to ignore
```

functions.name: rstcheck_core.inline_config.find_ignored_languages
documentation:
Search the rst source for rstcheck inline ignore-languages comments.

Languages are ignored via comment.

For example, to ignore C++, JSON, and Python:

```
.. testsetup::

    from rstcheck_core.inline_config import find_ignored_languages
```

```pycon
>>> list(find_ignored_languages('''
... Example
... =======
...
... .. rstcheck: ignore-languages=cpp,json
...
... .. rstcheck: ignore-languages=python
... ''', "<string>"))
['cpp', 'json', 'python']
```

```
param source

Rst source code

param source_origin

Origin of the source with the inline ignore comments

return

None

yield

Found languages to ignore
```

documentation:
Load config from file and merge with current config.

If the loaded file config overwrites the current config depends on the
`self.overwrite_config` attribute set on initialization.

```
param config_path

Path to config file; can be directory or file

param warn_unknown_settings

If a warning should be logged for unknown settings in config
file;
defaults to 
```

methods.name: rstcheck_core.runner.RstcheckMainRunner.update_file_list
documentation:
Update file path list with paths specified on initialization.

Uses paths from `RstcheckMainRunner.check_paths`, resolves all file paths and
saves them in {py:attr}`RstcheckMainRunner.files_to_check`.

If a given path does not exist, it is filtered out and saved in
{py:attr}`RstcheckMainRunner.files_to_check`.

Clear the current file list. Then get the file and directory paths specified with
`self.check_paths` attribute set on initialization and search them for rst files
to check. Add those files to the file list.

methods.name: rstcheck_core.runner.RstcheckMainRunner._filter_nonexisting_paths
documentation:
Filter nonexisting paths out.

If recursive is not active only files are allowed, else directories are also allowed.

```
param paths

List of paths to filter

return

Filtered path list
```

```
return

List of lists of errors found per file
```

methods.name: rstcheck_core.runner.RstcheckMainRunner._run_checks_parallel
documentation:
Check all files from the file list in parallel and return the errors.

```
return

List of lists of errors found per file
```

methods.name: rstcheck_core.runner.RstcheckMainRunner._update_results
documentation:
Take results and update error cache.

Result normally come from {py:meth}`RstcheckMainRunner._run_checks_sync` or
{py:meth}`RstcheckMainRunner._run_checks_parallel`.
\:param results: List of lists of errors found

methods.name: rstcheck_core.runner.RstcheckMainRunner.check
documentation:
Check all files in the file list and save the errors.

Multiple files are run in parallel.

A new call overwrite the old cached errors.

methods.name: rstcheck_core.runner.RstcheckMainRunner.print_result
documentation:
Print all cached error messages and return exit code.

```
param output_file

file to print to; defaults to sys.stderr (if None)

return

exit code 0 if no error is printed; 1 if any error is printed
```

methods.name: rstcheck_core.runner.RstcheckMainRunner.run
documentation:
Run checks, print error messages and return the result.

```
return

exit code 0 if no error is printed; 1 if any error is printed
```

documentation:
Search, load, parse and validate rstcheck config from a directory.

Searches files from {py:data}`CONFIG_FILES` in the directory. If a file is found, try to load
the config from it. If is has no config, search further.

```

Directory to search

param log_missing_section_as_warning

If a missing config section in a config file should be
logged at WARNING () or INFO () level;
defaults to 

param warn_unknown_settings

If a warning should be logged for unknown settings in config file;
defaults to 

return

instance of  or
 if no file is found or no file has a rstcheck section
or NONE is passed as the config path.
```

functions.name: rstcheck_core.config.load_config_file_from_dir_tree
documentation:
Search, load, parse and validate rstcheck config from a directory tree.

Searches files from {py:data}`CONFIG_FILES` in the directory. If a file is found, try to load
the config from it. If is has no config, search further. If no config is found in the directory
search its parents one by one.

```
param dir_path

Directory to search

param log_missing_section_as_warning

defaults to 

param warn_unknown_settings

defaults to 

return

 if no file is found or no file has a rstcheck section
or NONE is passed as the config path.
```

documentation:

```
param config_base

The base config to merge into
```

modules.name: rstcheck_core.checker
documentation: Checking functionality.

functions.name: rstcheck_core.checker.check_file
documentation:
Check the given file for issues.

On every call docutils' caches for roles and directives are cleared by reloading their modules.

```
param source_file

Path to file to check

param rstcheck_config

Main configuration of the application

param overwrite_with_file_config

If the loaded file config should overwrite the
rstcheck_config;
defaults to 

return

A list of found issues
```

functions.name: rstcheck_core.checker._load_run_config
documentation:
Load file specific config file and create run config.

If the `rstcheck_config` does not contain a `config_path` the `source_file_dir` directory
tree is searched for a config file to load and merge into the `rstcheck_config`. The merge
strategy is set via `overwrite_config`.

```
param source_file_dir

Directory of the current file to check

param rstcheck_config

Main configuration of the application

param overwrite_config

If the loaded config should overwrite the rstcheck_config;
defaults to 

return

Merged config
```

functions.name: rstcheck_core.checker._get_source
documentation:
Get source from file or stdin.

If the file name is "-" then stdin is read for input instead of a file.

```
param source_file

return

Loaded content
```
